name: Butter-Poached Scallop with Corn Silk Velouté, Fermented Corn Emulsion & Citrus Ash Tuile
description: A Michelin-level scallop starter featuring gently butter-poached diver scallops, silky corn silk velouté, a lightly fermented corn emulsion, and a crisp citrus ash tuile. A refined balance of sweetness, umami, acidity, and texture using modernist techniques.
keywords: scallops, fine dining seafood, modernist cuisine, corn velouté, fermented corn, butter poached scallops, citrus ash tuile

steps.instruction: Ferment corn: Vacuum seal corn kernels with salt and koji. Ferment at room temperature for 48 hours. Blend with neutral oil until smooth, then strain into a glossy emulsion.
Prepare corn silk velouté: Roast corn cobs until golden. Simmer cobs, corn silk, kernels, and stock for 30 minutes. Strain, then blend with butter and cream until silky smooth. Season lightly.
Make citrus ash: Burn dried lemon and orange peels until fully blackened. Grind into a fine ash powder.
Prepare citrus ash tuile: Mix water, flour, olive oil, citrus zest, and a pinch of citrus ash. Cook thin layers in a nonstick pan until lacy and crisp. Cool and reserve.
Infuse aromatic oil: Warm grapeseed oil to 60°C. Add basil stems and lemon peel. Infuse using an ultrasonic bath or steep for 1 hour. Strain until clear.
Butter-poach scallops: Melt butter with kombu and garlic. Maintain temperature at 50–55°C. Add scallops and poach gently for 6–8 minutes until just set and translucent.
Plating: Spoon corn velouté onto the plate as a base. Place scallops slightly off-center. Add dots of fermented corn emulsion. Position a shard of citrus ash tuile vertically for height.
Finish: Drizzle aromatic oil in small droplets. Garnish with micro herbs and edible flowers. Season lightly with flaky salt and serve immediately.

winePairings.name: White Burgundy (Chardonnay)
winePairings.notes: Rich yet mineral-driven, complementing the butter-poached scallops and corn sweetness.

winePairings.name: Champagne (Brut Blanc de Blancs)
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and fine bubbles cut through the richness while enhancing the dish’s delicate textures.
